message:
fix(core): extract try_next_from_receiver and fix off-by-one lagged retry bound

The FilteredEventStream::try_next() method had the retry logic
inlined, making it untestable in isolation. Additionally, the lagged
retry bound used >= which caused the function to bail one probe early:
after exactly MAX_LAGGED_RETRIES (3) lagged markers, the next recv
call was never attempted, even though a valid event might be waiting
immediately after the burst of lag notifications.

Changes:
- Extract try_next_from_receiver() as a standalone function that
  accepts a recv closure, enabling unit tests with deterministic
  event sequences
- Fix the bound check from >= to > so that the function performs
  one final probe after the Nth lagged marker before giving up
  (3 lagged markers → 4 recv attempts total, up from 3)
- Refactor try_next() to delegate to the extracted function

Tests:
- filtered_stream_try_next_allows_final_lagged_retry_probe: 3 lagged
  markers followed by a valid event → event is delivered (was lost
  with the old >= bound)
- filtered_stream_try_next_still_bails_after_excessive_lagged_retries:
  4 lagged markers followed by a valid event → returns None
  (confirms the bound still works)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
